title: Drug Use Disorder Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Drug Use Disorder Support Groups 14 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter At Risk Families Domestic Violence Issues Men Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous), and classes (smoking cessation and anger management). 231 Lyon Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Support Groups: -Men's Narcotics Anonymous: Monday 7pm-8pm -Narcotics Anonymous: Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday/Sunday 7pm-8pm -Wellbriety: Wednesday 6pm-7pm -Gamblers Anonymous: Tuesday 6:30pm-7:30pm -Women for Sobriety: TBD Email (503) 791-3411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE CHANCE CORVALLIS ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous). 456 SW Monroe Avenue Suite 108 Corvallis, OR 973335412 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies, call for current schedule Email (541) 224-2750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE CHANCE LEBANON ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, guidance, and resources to help those in recovery. Services offered include relapse prevention groups (Alcoholics Anonymous, Gamblers Anonymous, and Narcotics Anonymous). 365 S Main Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm, Friday 9am-3:30pm Email (541) 248-2750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE THE CHURCH ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Hosts the peer support group Women for Sobriety. 238 3rd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Friday noon Email (541) 791-3411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CRYSTAL METH ANONYMOUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about support groups and other services in regard to the topic of crystal methamphetamine addiction. Locator Eligibility: Individuals experiencing crystal methamphetamine addiction and their friends and family members Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (855) 638-4373 Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY ACCESSING SUCCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals struggling with substance use or those in recovery. Services include screenings and referrals to treatment providers or community partners as needed. Collaborates with child welfare and local Treatment Court programs. Offers peer support for individuals in recovery, affected family members, and families involved in other Relief Nursery services. 1720 W 25th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children age 6 and younger who are enrolled in other Relief Nursery services Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm. Evening classes available. (541) 343-9706 114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY ROBIN JAQUA CHILD AND FAMILY CENTER ACCESSING SUCCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals struggling with substance use or those in recovery. Services include screenings and referrals to treatment providers or community partners as needed. Collaborates with child welfare and local Treatment Court programs. Offers peer support for individuals in recovery, affected family members, and families involved in other Relief Nursery services. 850 S 42nd Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Families with children age 6 and younger who are enrolled in other Relief Nursery services Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm. Evening classes available. (541) 343-9706 114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health and substance abuse assessments. Specialists provide consultations, referrals, support and resources to staff, and group therapy, such as Seeking Safety. All consultations and assessments are confidential, except when there's a risk of harm to oneself or others, or in cases of child, disabled person, or elder abuse. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing the impacts of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assult Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING HOPE CHURCH LIVING FREE MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Christian faith-based support groups and classes for individuals experiencing addiction to alcohol, drugs, food, or pornography, as well as for those dealing with anger, depression, grief, and divorce. Offers Beyond the Walls, a class for families with loved ones who are incarcerated. Child care is available for support groups and classes. Also provides a community meal. Worship services are offered but are not required for participation. 2711 NE Andresen Road Vancouver, WA 98661 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Community meal: Tuesday 5:30pm -Support groups: 7:20pm, support group times vary -Worship service: Tuesday 6:30pm-7:20pm Email (360) 944-3905 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARIJUANA ANONYMOUS WORLD SERVICES MARIJUANA ANONYMOUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 12-Step program modeled after Alcoholics Anonymous to help those with a desire to stop using marijuana. Website provides listings for local meetings by state. Online meetings are also available. 5551 Hollywood Boulevard #1043 Hollywood, CA 900286814 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (800) 766-6779 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NARCOTICS ANONYMOUS WORLD SERVICES NARCOTICS ANONYMOUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 12-step program offering support groups for individuals who would like to maintain a drug-free lifestyle. Does not focus on any particular drug and makes no distinction between alcohol and drugs. The website provides a search tool to find local meetings. Website Van Nuys, CA 91409 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low barrier treatment services for drug and alcohol addiction, assessment, treatment planning, individual/group counseling, case management, peer support, mentoring, specimen testing, supported employment, and housing resources. 4585 SW 185th Avenue Aloha, OR 97078 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday - Thursday 9am to 7pm and Friday 9am to 5pm Email (503) 591-9280 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES HILLSBORO BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides low barrier treatment services for drug and alcohol addiction, assessment, treatment planning, individual/group counseling, case management, peer support, mentoring, specimen testing, supported employment, and housing resources. 395 W Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday - Thursday 9am to 7pm and Friday 9am to 5pm Email (503) 591-9280 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON MEN'S RESIDENTIAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A 54-bed drug and alcohol residential treatment facility for men. The six month residency consists of intensive, tightly structured treatment in which a therapeutic relationship with MRC counselors will be fostered. Additional six month non-residency continuing care is also offered. Hosts Stepping Up meetings to explore the 12 steps, which are open to members of the community who are in recovery. 2318 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Residential services are for men involved in the Criminal Justice System. Meetings are open to male identifying members of the recovery community. Hours: Residential Services: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Meetings: Call for upcoming meetings times (503) 235-8655 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS CHANCE ADDICTIONS/DEPENDENCIES SUPPORT GROUPS EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY ACCESSING SUCCESS EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY ACCESSING SUCCESS GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK SEQUOIA MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE NETWORK VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON MEN'S RESIDENTIAL CENTER
